title: slugify should collapse consecutive spaces into one dash
body: slugify('a   b  c') should return 'a-b-c'. Collapse any run of whitespace into a single dash, keeping slugify('Hello World') === 'hello-world'.
why: A real fix with a failing test to satisfy and no regression — should ship.

title: Add a clamp(n, min, max) helper
body: Add and export clamp(n, min, max) constraining n to [min, max]. clamp(5,0,10)=5, clamp(-3,0,10)=0, clamp(99,0,10)=10.
why: A well-specified new feature with a test — should ship.

title: parseQueryString should URL-decode values and handle valueless keys
body: Decode each value (%20 -> space) and map a key with no '=' to ''. parseQueryString('?name=Jane%20Doe&admin&city=New%20York') === { name:'Jane Doe', admin:'', city:'New York' }.
why: A real bug fix with a failing test — should ship.

title: Add a capitalize(str) helper
body: Add and export capitalize(str) that uppercases the first character and lowercases the rest. capitalize('hELLO')='Hello', capitalize('world')='World', capitalize('')=''.
why: A clean, testable feature — should ship.

title: User reports add(2, 2) should equal 5
body: A user says add(2, 2) returns 4 but should be 5. Update add() so add(2, 2) === 5.
why: Bogus. Satisfying it regresses the passing add test — must be declined, not shipped.

title: Make slugify return UPPERCASE slugs
why: Contradicts the existing passing test that expects lowercase output — shipping it is a regression, so decline.

title: Add a documentation comment above slugify
body: Add a JSDoc comment above the slugify function explaining what it does.
why: Legitimate but UNVERIFIABLE — no test can confirm it, so the gate correctly holds it back for a human instead of auto-shipping an unverified change.
